content:
# Combined LUT + CUDA 3D Texture Grading — Design Decision (2026-04-03)

## What
Replace 3-kernel GPU grading pipeline (lut_apply → hsl_correct → clarity) with a single
precomputed combined LUT sampled via CUDA 3D texture objects. Clarity hard-deleted.
Expected: ~15ms/frame at 4K vs 332ms current (~22× speedup on grading stage).

## Key Decisions

### Clarity: hard delete
No flag, no fallback. Maxine handles sharpening when implemented.

### Grid size: 97³ (COMBINED_LUT_GRID constant)
Empirically anchored on issue #25 validation (65³: p99=0.81/255, max=2.61/255).
97³ gives p99=0.36/255 = 1.4 10-bit LSBs — borderline-safe for future 10-bit footage.
33³ fails 10-bit (p99=13 LSBs). 65³ risky (p99=3.2 LSBs). 129³ safe but 129MB VRAM.
Bump constant to 129 if empirical 10-bit testing reveals banding.

### Depth bands: reuse calibration zones (5 adaptive)
Same soft triangular blend as current lut_apply.cu. No resampling step, no extra error.

### GPU LUT build (not CPU rayon)
Build kernel writes to device buffer → cudaMemcpy3D device→cudaArray3D.
Avoids 55MB PCIe roundtrip. Build time <1ms GPU vs ~34ms CPU rayon.
grade_pixel.cuh: shared __device__ function used by build kernel only.

### u8 I/O in per-frame kernel
Kernel reads u8 pixels directly, converts to f32 internally, writes u8 output.
Eliminates CPU u8→f32 expansion (~10ms) and 75MB htod overhead per frame.

### float4 texture format
Single tex3D<float4> fetch returns all 3 channels from hardware-interpolated lookup.

### Calibration + params baked at CudaGrader construction
CudaGrader::new(calibration, params) — not passed per-call.
grade_frame_with_grader signature drops calibration + params arguments.

## Per-frame data movement at 4K
Before: CPU expand 10ms + htod 133MB(9ms) + 3 kernels + 14 allocs + dtoh 100MB(7ms) = 332ms
After:  htod 58MB(4ms) + 1 kernel + 3 allocs + dtoh 25MB(2ms) = ~15ms

## Spec
